title: The Eastern Flank and Donald Trump II’s Policy
content:
In this episode, we examine how Donald Trump’s foreign policy has affected NATO’s Eastern Flank – from reactions to the military intervention in Venezuela and the Greenland episode – to efforts to strengthen defence capabilities.

Interested in first-hand perspectives on the European Union and the countries of the Eastern Flank? Tune in to our podcast. Each month, senior editors from Eastern Flank newsrooms come together to discuss issues shaping the region and the EU.

As always, the discussion is grounded in a monthly collaborative report co-authored by editors from across the Eastern Flank.

The post The Eastern Fla
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: European countries will have access to substantial funding to strengthen security – how will Latvia use it?
content:
In light of growing security risks in the region, the European Union (EU) has established a special funding programme to provide Member States with access to significant financial resources for strengthening defence. Latvia will also have access to substantial funds to enhance its military capabilities.

In recent years, European countries have invested considerable effort and significant financial resources in security and defence, while at the same time committing not to stop and to further increase their investments. The seriousness of the situation is underscored by the assessment of the European Commission (EC) that since the beginning of 2025, the EU’s security situation has significantly worsened. This is influenced not only by Russia’s war in
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: Who benefits from the EU-India agreement and who does not?
content:
At the end of January, the European Union and India concluded negotiations on the “mother of all agreements” – a historic, far-reaching and commercially significant free trade agreement, described as the largest of its kind “ever concluded by either side”.

“The agreement will strengthen economic and political ties between the world’s second- and fourth-largest economies at a time of growing geopolitical tension and global economic challenges,” the Representation of the European Commission (EC) in Latvia wrote in a press release announcing the deal.

Alongside this document, a Memorandum of Understanding on a comprehensive framework for mobility with India (EU-India Comprehensive Framework of Cooperation on Mobility) was also signed, aimed a
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: Does the Mercosur agreement threaten EU farmers? Exaggeration or real risk?
content:
The trade agreement between the European Union (EU) and the South American bloc Mercosur has, in recent months, sparked not only widespread discussion but also large-scale farmer protests.

Concerns have been raised about the EU market being flooded with cheaper and lower-quality imported agricultural products, which could harm or even put EU agricultural businesses out of business.

Critics have also argued that the proposed agricultural protection mechanisms are insufficient. The agreement is currently under review by the Court of Justice of the European Union to provide a legal opinion on its compliance with EU treaties.

However, the European Commission could decide on its provisional application, meaning that only those elemen
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: Disinformation Claims Czech President Is Part of Ukrainian Corruption Scheme. ‘It Was Too Sophisticated,’ Expert Says
content:
Czech president spent his holiday riding a motorcycle and staying in modest motels, yet claims appeared on social media that he was staying in a luxury hotel linked to the Ukrainian corrupted leadership. According to an expert, it was a carefully crafted and professionally presented piece of disinformation.

When President Petr Pavel travelled to Spain at the turn of January and February for a planned holiday, it was meant to be a private trip. Like several of his previous breaks, it involved touring the country by motorcycle with a group of friends.

This time, however, the getaway sparked a fabricated corruption claim that quickly spread across Czech social media.

One figure from the Czech disinfo
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: Beyond the Great Firewall: How the Chinese view Donald Trump and Europe
content:
Donald Trump evokes a mixture of admiration and irony in China – he is seen both as a tough player and an eccentric outsider. The Chinese view Europe and the Eastern flank through the lens of power, weakness, and their own strategic calculations.

From a Polish perspective, the phenomenon of Donald Trump remains a contentious issue. For some, he is a politician who brutally exposed the weaknesses of the liberal order. For others, he symbolizes chaos, unilateralism, and the undermining of Western foundations. In just a few months of his presidency, Trump managed to launch a trade war with China, openly name it the United States’ greatest rival, question the value of collective commitments under NATO, suggest taking over Greenland, and
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: From Ukrainian Helmets to Russian-Born Medalists: The Milan Games in Controversy
content:
The Winter Olympic Games in Milan and Cortina d’Ampezzo concluded on Sunday, but the controversies that surrounded them are likely to linger far longer than the Olympic flame itself. Beyond sporting triumphs, the Games were marked by political tensions – most notably those linked to Russia’s war against Ukraine.

From the outset, emotions ran high over the participation of Russian athletes, as well as public unease in Italy over the visible presence of ICE officers in host cities. Over the past two weeks, however, fresh scandals emerged, reaching well beyond the realm of sport and into the political arena.

A Ukrainian athlete’s helmet sparked a dispute between Kyiv and the International Olympic Committee (IOC). A medal won by an athlete of R
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: Not fixed yet, says Brussels about Druzhba pipeline – as Fico and Orbán insist otherwise
content:
The Slovak and Hungarian prime ministers claim that the key Ukrainian pipeline, damaged last month, has already been repaired, but Kyiv is deliberately blocking the delivery of Russian oil to them. Seeking revenge, they appear to ignore the official state of events presented by Kyiv and the European Commission.

Has the Druzhba been repaired? This question, concerning the recently damaged Ukrainian oil pipeline carrying Russian crude to Central Europe, has become a a key source of tension between Ukraine and the Slovak and Hungarian governments.

Both Robert Fico and Viktor Orbán claim that it has been fixed, arguing that Kyiv is refusing to resume the transit of Russian oil for political reasons. That is why they have
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: The Eastern Flank and Donald Trump II’s Policy – Infographic
content:
Most of the EU member states along the Union’s Eastern border have traditionally been staunch pro-American Atlanticists. Having endured decades of Soviet domination, they came to view the United States as the cornerstone of their security and a symbol of democracy, freedom and economic prosperity.

Donald Trump’s second presidential term has dealt a serious blow to transatlantic cooperation and left traditional Atlanticists in Eastern Europe struggling to find the right approach.

The post The Eastern Flank and Donald Trump II’s Policy – Info
creator: Olga Jurkowska

title: How Donald Trump gambles in Venezuela and Greenland shook the Eastern flank
content:
The U.S. military strike in Venezuela and President Trump’s push to acquire Greenland has left even long‑standing Atlanticist allies off‑balance. Nine EU Eastern flank states struggled to respond clearly – revealing deep unease about Washington’s direction and its implications for NATO and regional security.
